race.name: Engelbrektsloppet
race.tagline: Sixty kilometers through the mining country of Bergslagen — Sweden's second-largest ski race and a pillar of En Svensk Klassiker.

vitals.aid_stations: Service stations with warm drinks and energy food

climate.description: Bergslagen's inland position typically delivers reliable cold and good snow conditions in early February. Temperatures range from -15°C to -3°C. The forested terrain provides shelter from wind but can trap cold air in valleys.
challenges: Cold starts in shaded forest valleys
Variable snow conditions across elevation changes
Classic-only format demands precise wax choice
Low-altitude course vulnerable to mild winters

course.primary: Point-to-point through the historic Bergslagen mining district, rolling forest terrain with moderate elevation changes. The course passes through the cultural landscape of Sweden's iron-producing heartland.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through forest and open terrain
course.grooming: Well-groomed Swedish standard, classic tracks only
features: Second-largest cross-country ski race in Sweden
Part of En Svensk Klassiker (Swedish Classic) four-race challenge
Course through historic Bergslagen mining landscape
Named after medieval Swedish freedom fighter Engelbrekt Engelbrektsson
Rolling terrain through mixed forest and open countryside

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Historic Swedish race with deep cultural roots. The En Svensk Klassiker connection elevates its prestige. Modest elevation and predominantly domestic field keep the raw score moderate.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 grants 1-tier promotion from T3 to T2

history.summary: Founded in 1969 and named after Engelbrekt Engelbrektsson, a 15th-century Swedish rebel leader from the Bergslagen mining region. The race quickly established itself as Sweden's second-largest ski marathon behind Vasaloppet. Its inclusion in En Svensk Klassiker — a four-race endurance challenge combining skiing, cycling, running, and swimming — has cemented its place in Swedish sporting culture.
notable_facts: Named after Engelbrekt Engelbrektsson, who led a peasant revolt in 1434
Second-largest ski race in Sweden, behind only Vasaloppet
One of four events in En Svensk Klassiker (The Swedish Classic)
Course traverses the historic iron-mining region of Bergslagen
Many participants use it as a Vasaloppet warm-up race
